panels.title: Shooters — things to investigate
panels.content:
## Game state

Shooters — whether arcade-style shmups, run-and-gun games, or third-person action shooters — tend to have clear state separations between menu, active stage, results, and game over. A state index or flag set is worth finding early, as it scopes most other searches.

## Score and BCD

Older arcade-style shooters frequently store score in BCD (binary-coded decimal). If a score that should read as 1000 shows a different value in the memory viewer, BCD is worth investigating alongside standard integer formats.

## Ammo and weapons

Ammo counts may be per-weapon or shared across a slot system. When searching for ammo, fire exactly one shot and search immediately — other values change in the same frame as firing, and a larger window produces more noise. Each weapon may have its own ammo address or the active weapon ID may select which entry in an array is currently relevant.

## Health

Some shooters have no HP value — instead they use a single damage or death flag. One-hit-kill games will show a flag changing rather than a value decreasing. Both patterns are worth investigating.

## Timers

Timers in shooter stages may be a float, frame counter, integer in hex, BCD, centiseconds in a single address, or split across addresses for minutes and seconds. Search for a changing value during the active stage and investigate each format.

panels.title: Protection
panels.infobox: Identify these before writing achievement logic.

entry_Protection.name: Demo mode
entry_Protection.tNote: Check for a specific value that is set or cleared only on the main menu or when demo mode initiates. May be a standalone flag or part of a larger game state index.

entry_Protection.name: Save protection
entry_Protection.tNote: Find values that match a loading state or last loaded in-game state so that you can confirm the player is in-game for at least one frame by checking Delta = in-game.

entry_Protection.name: Cheat protection
entry_Protection.tNote: Investigate old guides or walkthroughs for possible cheats. If they offer an advantage, block them. Look for on/off flags, unnatural resource increases during a menu or pause scenario.

entry_Protection.name: Invincibility cheat
entry_Protection.tNote: If this exists, damageless achievements must require it to be off.

entry_Protection.name: Infinite ammo cheat
entry_Protection.tNote: If this exists, ammo-based challenge achievements must require it to be off.

entry_Protection.name: Infinite lives cheat
entry_Protection.tNote: If this exists, lives-based achievements must require it to be off.

entry_Protection.name: All weapons cheat
entry_Protection.tNote: If this exists, weapon collection achievements must require it to be off.

entry_Protection.name: Stage select / skip cheat
entry_Protection.tNote: If this exists, progression achievements must require it to be off.

entry_Protection.name: 2-player / co-op mode
entry_Protection.tNote: A second human player changes the game state. Investigate whether this needs to be excluded from solo achievements.

panels.title: Progression
panels.infobox: Values that track forward movement through missions, stages, and story.

entry_Progression.name: Game state / screen index
entry_Progression.tNote: A value that changes between the title screen, active stage, results screen, and game over.

entry_Progression.name: Stage / mission ID
entry_Progression.tNote: The identifier for the currently loaded stage or mission.

entry_Progression.name: Difficulty setting
entry_Progression.tNote: The selected difficulty. Scope anchor for difficulty-gated achievements.

entry_Progression.name: Stage completion flag
entry_Progression.tNote: A value that changes when the current stage ends cleanly.

entry_Progression.name: Mission completion flags
entry_Progression.tNote: Per-mission permanent completion records. May be a bitmask or individual bytes.

entry_Progression.name: Story / event flags
entry_Progression.tNote: Flags for cutscenes and story beats.

entry_Progression.name: Weapon unlock flags
entry_Progression.tNote: Which weapons have been permanently unlocked.

entry_Progression.name: Current objective / event ID
entry_Progression.tNote: The active mission objective or phase within the current stage.

entry_Progression.name: Game completion flag
entry_Progression.tNote: A value set when the credits roll or the final screen is reached.

entry_Progression.name: Clears per character
entry_Progression.tNote: If the game has multiple playable characters, where per-character completion is stored.

panels.title: Collections
panels.infobox: Things the player has permanently unlocked or accumulated.

entry_Collection.name: Weapon unlock flags
entry_Collection.tNote: Which weapons are available. May be a bitmask, individual flags, or a count.

entry_Collection.name: Upgrade level — weapon 0
entry_Collection.tNote: Current upgrade tier of the first weapon.

entry_Collection.name: Upgrade level — weapon 1
entry_Collection.tNote: Second weapon. Compare address offset from weapon 0.

entry_Collection.name: In-game currency / credits
entry_Collection.tNote: Accumulated currency used for weapon or upgrade purchases.

entry_Collection.name: Power-up inventory
entry_Collection.tNote: Stored power-ups held between stages, if the game has this system.

entry_Collection.name: Stage medal / rank
entry_Collection.tNote: Performance rating per stage. May be a letter grade, a tier value, or a medal type.

entry_Collection.name: Total medals count
entry_Collection.tNote: A running total of medals earned across all stages.

entry_Collection.name: Character unlock flags
entry_Collection.tNote: Which playable characters have been unlocked.

entry_Collection.name: Gallery / extra content flags
entry_Collection.tNote: Bonus content unlocked through score or completion milestones.

entry_Collection.name: High score — stage 0
entry_Collection.tNote: Personal best score for stage 0. Additional stages may follow an offset pattern.

panels.title: Challenges
panels.infobox: Values for achievements with pass/fail conditions.

entry_Challenge.name: Player HP (current)
entry_Challenge.tNote: Current health. The data type depends on the game's health system.

entry_Challenge.name: Player HP (max)
entry_Challenge.tNote: Maximum health. Useful for ratio-based logic.

entry_Challenge.name: Damage / death flag
entry_Challenge.tNote: A value that changes when damage is taken. In one-hit-kill games, this may be the only health-related value.

entry_Challenge.name: Invincibility / i-frames
entry_Challenge.tNote: A short-lived value active during post-hit recovery.

entry_Challenge.name: Player lives (current)
entry_Challenge.tNote: Lives currently in reserve.

entry_Challenge.name: Score
entry_Challenge.tNote: May be a standard integer, BCD, or a scaled value.

entry_Challenge.name: Active weapon ID
entry_Challenge.tNote: Which weapon is currently equipped.

entry_Challenge.name: Ammo count — primary weapon
entry_Challenge.tNote: Fire one shot and search immediately after.

entry_Challenge.name: Ammo count — secondary weapon
entry_Challenge.tNote: Same investigation method.

entry_Challenge.name: Ammo count — special weapon
entry_Challenge.tNote: Additional weapon slots follow the same pattern.

entry_Challenge.name: Enemy kill counter
entry_Challenge.tNote: A count of enemies defeated. May be global, per-type, or per-stage.

entry_Challenge.name: Boss HP (current)
entry_Challenge.tNote: Only valid during an active boss encounter.

entry_Challenge.name: Boss HP (max)
entry_Challenge.tNote: Useful for phase-threshold logic.

entry_Challenge.name: Combo / multiplier level
entry_Challenge.tNote: Current hit streak level.

entry_Challenge.name: Combo decay timer
entry_Challenge.tNote: Time remaining before the combo resets. May be a frame counter or a time value.

entry_Challenge.name: Stage timer
entry_Challenge.tNote: May be a float, frame counter, integer in hex, BCD, centiseconds in a single address, or split across minutes, seconds, and centiseconds.

entry_Challenge.name: Player X position
entry_Challenge.tNote: Horizontal coordinate. Useful for positional achievements and Easter eggs.

entry_Challenge.name: Player Y position
entry_Challenge.tNote: Vertical coordinate. In vertical shmups, this is the primary movement axis.

entry_Challenge.name: Checkpoint / respawn point
entry_Challenge.tNote: Which checkpoint is active for the current respawn.

entry_Challenge.name: Death counter
entry_Challenge.tNote: Deaths this run or total lifetime deaths.
